unkeyed_headers.description: Web cache poisoning via unkeyed headers — inject values into cached responses by abusing headers the cache ignores when computing the cache key

probes.description: X-Forwarded-Host injection — if evil.com appears in response body and cache headers present, the header is unkeyed and poisons cached content
X-Forwarded-Scheme downgrade — forces 301/302 redirect to HTTP, poisoning cache with redirect loop or protocol downgrade
X-Original-URL override — backend processes /admin instead of requested path; response length diff indicates path override worked

cache_deception.description: Web cache deception — trick the cache into storing authenticated responses by appending static file extensions to dynamic URLs

probes.description: Path confusion with .css extension — cache stores authenticated page content as static CSS resource, leaking sensitive data to unauthenticated users
Semicolon path parameter with .js extension — backend ignores semicolon suffix, cache keys on full path including .js and stores authenticated content
URL-encoded slash with .css extension — path normalization discrepancy between cache and backend causes authenticated content to be cached as static file

parameter_cloaking.description: Cache key manipulation via parameter cloaking — exploit cache parameter parsing discrepancies to inject unkeyed parameters into cached responses

probes.description: Semicolon parameter cloaking — cache excludes utm_content from key but backend parses semicolon as parameter delimiter, reflecting the evil parameter value in cached response
Fat GET request — send GET with body parameters; cache ignores request body when computing key but backend processes body params, poisoning cached response with attacker-controlled values
